meaning: n.鍩嬩紡,鍩嬩紡澶剉t.鍩嬩紡,浼忓嚮
examples: He felt that the police were there somewhere in ambuscade, waiting for the signal agreed upon and ready to stretch out their arm.
There are men who lie in ambuscade along the frontier, armed to the teeth, and whose business it is to throw difficulties in the way of transporting merchandise from the one country to the other.
